message:
feat(core): add Windows VFS backend to PagerBackend enum

Wire WindowsVfs through all PagerBackend dispatch arms (open, begin,
journal_mode, set_journal_mode, install_wal_backend, checkpoint,
cache_metrics) behind #[cfg(target_os = "windows")].  Also includes
e2e backoff fix for sub-millisecond granularity and formatting cleanup.

Fixes beads_rust#116

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1109,7 +1109,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3u7.1","title":"VFS trait abstraction & platform implementations","description":"VFS abstraction: Trait-based VFS with open/close/read/write/sync/lock/truncate/size. Platform implementations for Linux/macOS/Windows. File locking protocol compatible with SQLite. Tracing: span 'vfs_op' with fields op_type, file_path, bytes, duration_us. Log level: DEBUG per-operation, TRACE for lock acquisition. Metric: fsqlite_vfs_operations_total counter by op_type, fsqlite_vfs_io_bytes_total counter by direction(read/write), fsqlite_vfs_sync_duration_seconds histogram.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Trait contract satisfied on Linux and macOS. 2. File locking protocol compatible with SQLite. 3. Read/write operations correct under concurrent access. 4. Platform abstraction has zero unsafe outside membrane.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:05.332164549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:17:08.732559516Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:17:08.732540421Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensqlite","impl","vfs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3u7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1376,"issue_id":"bd-3u7.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"VFS trait abstraction complete (bd-3u7.1):

ALREADY EXISTED before this bead:
- Vfs trait + VfsFile trait (traits.rs, 344 lines)
- UnixVfs with POSIX fcntl locking (unix.rs, 2767 lines)
- WindowsVfs with sidecar file locking (windows.rs, 1412 lines)
- MemoryVfs for testing (memory.rs, 1466 lines)
- SHM support for WAL mode (shm.rs, 395 lines)
- Five-level SQLite lock protocol (None < Shared < Reserved < Pending < Exclusive)
- Host filesystem helpers (lib.rs)

ADDED by this bead:
- crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/metrics.rs: VFS observability module with:
  - TracingFile<F: VfsFile> wrapper emitting 'vfs_op' spans (DEBUG level)
  - Lock operations traced at TRACE level for finer granularity
  - Fields: op_type, file_path, bytes, duration_us, ok
  - VfsMetrics atomic counters: read/write/sync/lock/unlock/truncate/close/file_size ops
  - read_bytes_total, write_bytes_total byte counters
  - GLOBAL_VFS_METRICS singleton, MetricsSnapshot with Display
  - 6 unit tests all passing

Quality gates:
- cargo check --all-targets: PASS
- cargo clippy -p fsqlite-vfs --all-targets -- -D warnings: PASS
- cargo fmt -p fsqlite-vfs -- --check: PASS
- cargo test -p fsqlite-vfs -- metrics: 6 passed","created_at":"2026-02-13T19:17:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3u7.2","title":"io_uring async I/O backend","description":"io_uring backend (§15.7): Linux io_uring for async batched I/O. Submit multiple page reads/writes in single syscall. SQE/CQE ring management. Fallback to pread/pwrite on older kernels. Tracing: span 'iouring_batch' with fields sqe_count, cqe_count, batch_bytes. Log level: DEBUG batch submissions, INFO periodic throughput stats. Metric: fsqlite_iouring_submissions_total counter, fsqlite_iouring_batch_size histogram, fsqlite_iouring_throughput_bytes_per_sec gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. >= 2x throughput vs pread/pwrite on random 4KB reads. 2. Correct SQE/CQE handling. 3. Fallback to pread/pwrite on kernels < 5.10. 4. No resource leaks on shutdown.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:05.396727946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:22:19.670504742Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:22:19.669981352Z","close_reason":"Completed: Linux file-backed pager now defaults to io_uring with passing targeted and workspace quality gates (rch evidence in bead comments).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vfs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3u7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3u7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1377,"issue_id":"bd-3u7.2","author":"CreamRaven","text":"Implemented Linux io_uring integration through core pager selection: file-backed Connection::open now selects PagerBackend::IoUring on Linux (Memory remains unchanged; non-Linux Unix stays UnixVfs). Added connection-level tests: test_memory_connection_uses_memory_pager_backend and test_file_backed_connection_uses_iouring_pager_backend. Validation via rch: cargo fmt -p fsqlite-core -- --check (pass); cargo check -p fsqlite-core --all-targets (pass); cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib pager_backend -- --nocapture (2 passed). Also revalidated io_uring VFS unit slice via rch: cargo test -p fsqlite-vfs --lib uring::tests -- --nocapture (2 passed).","created_at":"2026-02-20T20:00:19Z"},{"id":1967,"issue_id":"bd-3u7.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-03-03 SilentMarsh: Restored Linux io_uring pager routing in fsqlite-core. Changes in crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs: PagerBackend now includes Linux IoUring variant; file-backed PagerBackend::open() selects IoUringVfs on Linux and UnixVfs on non-Linux Unix; backend-kind diagnostics/tests updated to report iouring on Linux and unix elsewhere. Validation (all via rch): cargo fmt -p fsqlite-core -- --check PASS; cargo check -p fsqlite-core --all-targets PASS; cargo test -p fsqlite-core --lib pager_backend -- --nocapture PASS (5 tests, including test_file_backed_connection_uses_iouring_pager_backend and backend-kind checks). Workspace gates: cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; cargo fmt --all -- --check currently FAILS due pre-existing formatting drift in unrelated files (crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_3u7_4_vfs_contract_fault_injection.rs, crates/fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs).","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:22:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3u7.3","title":"Test VFS with deterministic fault injection","description":"Test VFS for deterministic fault injection: In-memory VFS with configurable fault injection (read/write failures, latency injection, partial writes, disk full). Deterministic replay from seed. Used by franken_fault test harness. Tracing: span 'test_vfs_fault' with fields fault_type, trigger_count, file_path. Log level: DEBUG fault injection events. Metric: fsqlite_test_vfs_faults_injected_total counter by fault_type.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All fault types injectable (read/write failure, latency, partial write, disk full). 2. Deterministic replay from seed. 3. Fault trigger count configurable. 4. Used by all storage test suites.  8. Unit/property test coverage for this bead is mandatory, including deterministic seeds and explicit invariant checks. 9. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script must validate integrated behavior with replay instructions and archived artifacts. 10. Structured logging/metrics validation is required: trace/run/scenario IDs, timing, counters, and actionable failure context must be present and CI-verified.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CreamRaven","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:05.453862517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T17:36:53.850934641Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T17:36:53.850916227Z","close_reason":"Completed evidence","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vfs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3u7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3u7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3u7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3u7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1378,"issue_id":"bd-3u7.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed replay seed support and trigger-count controls.\n- Added structured tracing span 'test_vfs_fault' fields: fault_type, trigger_count, file_path.\n- Added metric name + per-fault counters: (1) VFS contract: verify all implementations satisfy trait contract (open/close/read/write/sync idempotency, lock exclusivity). (2) io_uring: benchmark vs pread/pwrite on random 4KB reads, target >= 2x throughput. (3) Fault injection VFS: verify all fault types are correctly injected and recoverable. (4) File locking: multi-process lock contention correctness. (5) Platform: run on Linux 5.10+ and macOS, verify identical behavior. Acceptance: VFS contract tests pass on all platforms, io_uring >= 2x throughput improvement.

 {"id":"bd-3udx","title":"Wire Connection PRAGMA journal_mode to pager/WAL backend","description":"Connection tracks journal mode pragma state but does not apply it to PagerBackend/SimplePager (set_journal_mode/set_wal_backend), leaving WAL path idle.  {"id":"bd-3uoj","title":"§17.9 Isomorphism Proof Template: Required for Optimization Correctness","description":"E2E Test:
- test_e2e_bd_3uoj_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.

## SUMMARY
Defines the isomorphism proof template required for every performance optimization that touches query execution or data storage. Every PR description for such changes MUST include a structured proof that the optimization preserves behavioral equivalence with the pre-optimization code. The template covers ordering preservation, tie-breaking behavior, float behavior, RNG seed stability, and Oracle fixture pass status. This ensures FrankenSQLite stays fast without drifting from parity with C SQLite. "It feels faster" is not an acceptable justification.

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS
- **Isomorphism Proof Template:** Required in PR description for every perf optimization touching query execution or data storage.
  ```
  Change: <description of optimization>
  - Ordering preserved:     [yes/no] (+why)
  - Tie-breaking unchanged: [yes/no] (+why)
  - Float behavior:         [identical / N/A]
  - RNG seeds:              [unchanged / N/A]
  - Oracle fixtures:        PASS (list reference case IDs)
  ```
- **Ordering Preserved:** The optimization must not change the order of result rows for queries with ORDER BY, or the order of side effects (inserts, updates, deletes) within a transaction.
- **Tie-Breaking Unchanged:** When ORDER BY does not fully determine order (ties exist), the tie-breaking behavior must remain identical. This is critical for golden output stability.
- **Float Behavior:** Floating-point arithmetic results must be bit-identical (not "close enough") unless explicitly documented as an accepted divergence.
- **RNG Seeds:** If the code uses any deterministic RNG (e.g., for hash table probing, sampling), the seed derivation must be unchanged so that deterministic tests produce identical outputs.
- **Oracle Fixtures:** The optimization MUST pass all relevant Oracle conformance fixtures. Specific case IDs must be listed.
- **Relationship to §17.8:** This template is referenced by the Extreme Optimization Loop (§17.8.1 step 3: PROVE) and Golden Checksums (§17.8.6). The golden checksum pass is the mechanical verification; the proof template is the human-readable justification.

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS
- INV-ISO-1: Every PR with a perf optimization touching query execution or data storage MUST include the isomorphism proof template in the PR description.
- INV-ISO-2: "Ordering preserved" MUST be answered yes/no with justification.
- INV-ISO-3: "Tie-breaking unchanged" MUST be answered yes/no with justification.
- INV-ISO-4: "Float behavior" MUST be answered identical or N/A.
- INV-ISO-5: "RNG seeds" MUST be answered unchanged or N/A.
- INV-ISO-6: "Oracle fixtures" MUST list specific reference case IDs that PASS.
- INV-ISO-7: "It feels faster" is NOT an acceptable justification for any optimization.

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
- `test_isomorphism_ordering_preserved`: Apply a known optimization (e.g., index scan rewrite). Verify result row order is identical to pre-optimization for all ORDER BY queries in the fixture set.
- `test_isomorphism_tie_breaking_unchanged`: Run queries with ties in ORDER BY (e.g., ORDER BY a where multiple rows have same a). Verify tie-breaking order is identical pre/post optimization.
- `test_isomorphism_float_behavior_identical`: Run float-producing queries (arithmetic, aggregates, math functions). Verify bit-identical results pre/post optimization.
- `test_isomorphism_rng_seeds_unchanged`: Run deterministic operations that depend on RNG seeds. Verify identical outputs pre/post optimization.
- `test_isomorphism_oracle_fixtures_pass`: Run full Oracle conformance fixture set after applying optimization. Verify all cases PASS.
- `test_isomorphism_golden_checksums_match`: Capture golden checksums pre-optimization. Apply optimization. Verify checksums match (sha256sum -c).
- `test_isomorphism_pr_template_present`: (CI check) Verify that PRs touching query/storage code include the isomorphism proof template in the PR description.

- test_e2e_bd_3uoj_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.
Take a concrete optimization (e.g., B-tree leaf page binary search replacing linear scan). Capture golden outputs for all Oracle conformance fixtures before the optimization. Apply the optimization. Re-run all Oracle fixtures and verify identical outputs. Compute golden checksums before and after and verify match. Fill in the isomorphism proof template and verify all fields are answered. Run the full Criterion benchmark suite to quantify the performance improvement alongside the behavioral equivalence proof.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Isomorphism proof template defined and documented in contributing guidelines.
- CI enforces presence of proof template in PRs that touch query execution or data storage paths.
- All 5 proof fields (ordering, tie-breaking, float, RNG, Oracle fixtures) must be explicitly answered.
- Golden checksum verification (sha256sum -c) passes for every optimization PR.
- Oracle conformance fixture suite runs as part of the proof verification.
- No optimization lands without the proof template — the gate is non-negotiable.
- "It feels faster" rejected as justification by CI linter or code review bot.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:04:52.550927826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:26:08.562525456Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T00:26:08.562490901Z","close_reason":"Validated isomorphism-proof template compliance via cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_3uoj_isomorphism_proof_template_compliance (20/20 passing)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uoj","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uoj","depends_on_id":"bd-bca.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1381,"issue_id":"bd-3uoj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §17.9 Isomorphism Proof Template (from P2 bd-2de5)

Required for every performance optimization PR:
- Ordering preserved, tie-breaking unchanged, float behavior, RNG seeds, oracle fixtures PASS.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:23:10Z"},{"id":1382,"issue_id":"bd-3uoj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §17.9 Isomorphism Proof Template

### Spec Content (Lines 16992-17005)

For every performance optimization that touches query execution or data storage, the PR description MUST include the isomorphism proof template:

```
Change: <description of optimization>
- Ordering preserved:     [yes/no] (+why)
- Tie-breaking unchanged: [yes/no] (+why)
- Float behavior:         [identical / N/A]
- RNG seeds:              [unchanged / N/A]
- Oracle fixtures:        PASS (list reference case IDs)
```

This ensures the project stays fast without drifting from parity. "It feels faster" is not an acceptable justification.

The template requires five fields:
1. **Ordering preserved:** Whether the optimization maintains the same row ordering for all queries (especially important for ORDER BY and GROUP BY)
2. **Tie-breaking unchanged:** Whether deterministic tie-breaking behavior is preserved (e.g., when two rows have equal sort keys, their relative order must remain the same as before)
3. **Float behavior:** Whether floating-point arithmetic produces identical results (bit-for-bit), or N/A if the optimization does not affect float paths
4. **RNG seeds:** Whether any internal RNG seeds are unchanged (relevant for probabilistic algorithms like sampling in ANALYZE), or N/A
5. **Oracle fixtures:** All conformance fixtures from §17.7 pass against C SQLite Oracle; specific case IDs must be listed

This is tied to §17.8.6 golden checksums: any perf-only change must produce identical golden output (sha256sum match).

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_isomorphism_ordering_preserved: After optimization, all ORDER BY queries produce identical row ordering to baseline
2. test_isomorphism_tie_breaking: Equal sort keys maintain same relative order before and after optimization
3. test_isomorphism_float_identical: Floating-point arithmetic produces bit-for-bit identical results before and after optimization
4. test_isomorphism_rng_seeds_unchanged: Internal RNG seeds produce same sequence before and after optimization
5. test_isomorphism_oracle_fixtures_pass: All conformance fixtures pass against C SQLite Oracle after optimization
6. test_isomorphism_golden_checksum_match: sha256sum of golden outputs matches before and after perf-only change
7. test_isomorphism_proof_template_required: PR CI gate rejects perf PRs without isomorphism proof template in description
8. test_isomorphism_no_vibes_optimization: Optimization without profiling evidence (named hotspot, Score >= 2.0) is rejected
9. test_isomorphism_group_by_stability: GROUP BY produces identical grouping and aggregate results before and after
10. test_isomorphism_explain_plan_unchanged: EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output is identical or strictly better (fewer steps) after optimization
11. test_isomorphism_commit_marker_artifacts: CommitMarker/CommitProof/AbortWitness artifacts identical before and after perf change
12. test_isomorphism_conformance_full_suite: Full conformance suite (1000+ golden files) passes after optimization

### E2E Test
End-to-end validation: For any performance optimization PR, run the complete isomorphism verification pipeline: (1) Capture golden outputs from baseline commit (sha256sum all golden_outputs/*). (2) Apply optimization. (3) Re-run all conformance fixtures and verify golden checksum match. (4) Verify ordering preserved for all ORDER BY queries in the test suite. (5) Verify tie-breaking unchanged for equal sort keys. (6) Verify float behavior is bit-for-bit identical. (7) Verify RNG seed sequences are unchanged. (8) Verify EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output is identical or strictly improved. (9) Verify CommitMarker/CommitProof/AbortWitness artifacts are identical. (10) Confirm the isomorphism proof template is present in the PR description with all 5 fields filled. This pipeline runs as a CI gate that blocks merge of any perf-touching PR.

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: emit stage-level progress and key branch decisions for this bead scope.
- INFO: emit summary counters and completion status for this bead scope.
- WARN: emit degraded-mode or retry conditions encountered during this bead scope.
- ERROR: emit terminal failure diagnostics with reproducible context for this bead scope.
- Reference: bd-1fpm structured repro-bundle logging conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit test identifiers for this bead are concrete and executable.
- [ ] E2E identifier for this bead is concrete and executable.
- [ ] Logging requirements include DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR and bd-1fpm alignment.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:10:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uzh","title":"5E.4: Implement SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT retry on page conflict","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:52:03.784178519Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:39:44.579568388Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T15:39:44.579501563Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uzh","depends_on_id":"bd-1xo1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T22:57:56Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1386,"issue_id":"bd-3uzh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# 5E.4: Implement SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT retry on page conflict

## What

When a concurrent transaction fails to commit due to first-committer-wins conflict or SSI
pivot detection, the error is `MvccError::BusySnapshot`. The application can then:

1. Roll back the transaction
2. Retry with a fresh snapshot (re-read, re-compute, re-commit)

This task implements the retry mechanism at the Connection level, including:
- Proper error propagation as SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT result code
- The adaptive retry policy (Beta-Bernoulli backoff model)
- `busy_handler` / `busy_timeout` PRAGMA support for configuring retry behavior

## Current state

The retry infrastructure is implemented in `crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/retry_policy.rs`:

```rust
pub enum RetryAction {
    FailNow,                           // Surface SQLITE_BUSY immediately
    RetryAfter { wait_ms: u64 },       // Retry after waiting
}

pub struct BetaPosterior {
    pub alpha: f64,    // Pseudo-count of successes
    pub beta: f64,     // Pseudo-count of failures
}

pub struct ContentionBucketKey {
    pub n_active_quantized: u8,      // Active writers (quantized)
    pub m2_hat_quantized: u8,        // Collision mass estimate
}

pub struct RetryCostParams {
    pub c_fail: f64,    // Cost of surfacing SQLITE_BUSY
    pub c_try: f64,     // Cost of one retry attempt
}
```

The `MvccError` variants (lifecycle.rs:185):
- `Busy` — lock contention (page lock held by another txn)
- `BusySnapshot` — stale snapshot (first-committer-wins conflict or SSI abort)

But Connection doesn't handle these error codes — it just propagates them as generic errors.

## Where to change

**Primary:** `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` — error handling in execute_commit()
and DML execution (for page lock contention during writes)

**Secondary:** `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` — PRAGMA busy_timeout / busy_handler support

## Implementation approach

### Error propagation

1. Map `MvccError::Busy` → `SqliteError::Busy` (SQLITE_BUSY, result code 5)
2. Map `MvccError::BusySnapshot` → `SqliteError::BusySnapshot` (SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT, extended code 517)
3. These must be distinct from generic errors so applications can pattern-match on them

### Retry at DML level (page lock contention)

When `write_page()` returns `MvccError::Busy` during INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE:
- Do NOT automatically retry at the DML level
- Surface SQLITE_BUSY to the application immediately
- Application decides whether to retry the statement or abort the transaction
- This matches C SQLite behavior: SQLITE_BUSY during DML = statement failed, txn still active

### Retry at commit level (first-committer-wins / SSI)

When `concurrent_commit()` returns `MvccError::BusySnapshot`:
- If `busy_handler` is set, invoke it
- If `busy_timeout` is set, retry with backoff up to timeout
- Use the `RetryPolicy` adaptive model:
  - Track success/failure rates per contention bucket
  - `BetaPosterior` estimates success probability
  - Decision: retry if expected cost of retry < expected cost of failure
- If all retries exhausted, surface SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT to application

### PRAGMA support

- `PRAGMA busy_timeout = N` — set maximum milliseconds to retry (0 = no retry)
- `PRAGMA busy_handler = ...` — C SQLite allows callback; we'll support timeout-based only
  initially, with a hook point for future callback support

## C SQLite reference

C SQLite's busy handling:
- `sqlite3_busy_handler(db, callback, arg)` — register callback invoked on BUSY
- `sqlite3_busy_timeout(db, ms)` — built-in handler that sleeps with exponential backoff
- SQLITE_BUSY (5) — lock contention, statement can be retried
- SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT (517) — snapshot too old, must restart transaction
- The handler is NOT called for SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT in C SQLite — it requires full txn restart

We follow the same semantics: busy_handler only applies to SQLITE_BUSY (page lock contention),
not SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT (which requires transaction restart, not simple retry).

## Tests

- `test_busy_snapshot_error_on_fcw_conflict`: Two concurrent txns write same page, second commit → BusySnapshot
- `test_busy_error_on_page_lock`: Two concurrent txns, second writes locked page → Busy
- `test_busy_timeout_retries`: Set busy_timeout, create contention → retries until timeout
- `test_busy_timeout_zero_no_retry`: busy_timeout=0, contention → immediate BUSY
- `test_busy_snapshot_not_retriable_in_place`: BusySnapshot → must restart transaction, not just retry commit
- `test_error_codes_distinct`: Busy vs BusySnapshot have different SqliteError variants
